---
title: "Downstream_MAGeCK_Analysis"
author: "Jonathan Chan"
date: '`r format(Sys.Date())`'
output: 
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
bibliography:
  - Bibliography.bib
---
#! indicates that you need to modify this code in order to tailor to other analyses.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#Loading packages
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(MAGeCKFlute)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(reshape2)
library(viridis)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggsignif)
#library(showtext)
library(scales)

working_directory <- getwd()
working_directory <- paste(working_directory,'dipg_screen_data', sep='/') #! Need to define your working directory folder here

#Importing Futura BKBT Font
#font_add(family = "futura_bkt", regular = "./Futura-Bk-BT-Book_18979.ttf")
# turn on shoCONext
#showtext_auto()

#Setting theme for ggplot to classic - You can change your plot text font and size here
theme_set(
  theme_classic()
  + theme(text = element_text(family = 'sans', size=14))
  ) 

#This function is for the generation of plots to export several ggplot objects into single pdf
multiple_plot_exporter <- function(input_plot_list, pdf_name){
  
  pdf(paste(pdf_name,'.pdf', sep=''), onefile=TRUE)
  for (i in seq_along(input_plot_list)){
    print(input_plot_list[[i]])
  }
  dev.off()
}
#This function is for the import of MAGeCK derived gene_summary.txt files
gene_summary_importer <- function(filename, working_directory){
  gene_summary <- as_tibble(read_delim(paste(working_directory,filename, sep='/')))
  
  return(gene_summary)
}

#This imports the list of common essential genes from DepMap's Feb22 release
common_essentials <- read_csv(paste(working_directory,'library_data/CRISPR_common_essentials_depmap_feb22.csv',sep='/')) %>%
  mutate(gene = str_extract(gene,'[^ (]*'))

#This imports the list of coding tumour suppressor genes from TSGene Database (6-05-2022 Version)
tsgenes <- read_csv(paste(working_directory,'library_data/TSGene_coding_tsg.csv', sep='/')) %>%
  rename(gene = `Gene Name`)



```

# Downstream Analysis of MAGeCK-MLE via MAGeCK-FLUTE Pipeline

This notebook outlines QC at the sequencing; read; sample; gene-level as per guidelines from @Li2015 as well as functional analysis via the MAGeCK-Flute R package from @Wang2019.

Below code imports the count data.
```{r import_counts}

count_summary <- as_tibble(read_delim(paste(working_directory,"count1b.countsummary.txt",sep='/'))) %>%
  #Appends a CellLine column to distinguish between two cell lines. 
  mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(Label,'CON')~'K27M', 
                              str_detect(Label, 'KO')~'K27M-KO', 
                              str_detect(Label, 'Plasmid')~'Plasmid')) %>% 
  #!Converts CellLine column into a factor (i.e an ordered list) 
  mutate(CellLine = factor(CellLine, levels=c('Plasmid','K27M','K27M-KO'))) %>%
  #Appends a Replicate column to indicate which sample is from either replicate 1 or replicate 2
  mutate(Replicate = case_when(str_detect(Label,'r1|Plasmid')~'1', str_detect(Label, 'r2')~'2')) %>%
  #Converts the Label column in count_summary.txt into a factor (i.e ordered list)
  mutate(Label = factor(Label, levels=c('Plasmid','CON_r1_d0','CON_r1_d11','CON_r1_d21','CON_r2_d0','CON_r2_d11','CON_r2_d21','KO_r1_d0','KO_r1_d11','KO_r1_d21','KO_r2_d0','KO_r2_d11','KO_r2_d21'), ordered=TRUE))

#! This imports the raw count files from MAGeCK
raw_counts <- read_delim(paste(working_directory, 'count1b.count.txt', sep='/'))
norm_counts <- read_delim(paste(working_directory, 'count1b.count_normalized.txt',sep='/'))

#! This defines the samplenames for all your samples i.e the labelsfrom count_summary.txt, as well as for each replicate.
samplenames <- c('Plasmid','CON_r1_d0','CON_r2_d0','CON_r1_d11','CON_r2_d11','CON_r1_d21','CON_r2_d21','KO_r1_d0','KO_r2_d0','KO_r1_d11','KO_r2_d11','KO_r1_d21','KO_r2_d21')
rep1_samples <- c(#'Plasmid',
                  'CON_r1_d0','CON_r1_d11','CON_r1_d21','KO_r1_d0','KO_r1_d11','KO_r1_d21')
rep2_samples <- c(#'Plasmid',
                  'CON_r2_d0','CON_r2_d11','CON_r2_d21','KO_r2_d0','KO_r2_d11','KO_r2_d21')
```
## Quality Control: Read Level

This uses the output of mageck count to assess numerous QC measures at the read level:

- Total number of sequencing reads
- Total number of mapped reads to gRNA library
  - Expected to be >300 x number of gRNAs
- % of total reads mapped
  - Expected to be > 65%
- Number of sgRNAs with zero read count
  - Low number in the plasmid library or early time points
  - Expected at most 1% of total sgRNAs
- Gini index of read count distribution (a measure of unevenness of distribution between samples)
  - Expected at most 0.1 for plasmid library & early time points; 0.2 for negative selection samples
- Distribution of the reads over the different gRNAs
  - Expected to have an even distribution across the different gRNAs across all samples


It also uses a threshold derived from the distribution plot of normalised read counts to remove outliers.
This has been chosen as 3000 from observation.
  
```{r read QC-mapping}
mapping_ratio_QC <- function(input_counts, plasmid){
  
  if (plasmid == FALSE){
    input_counts <- filter(input_counts,Label != 'Plasmid')
  }
  
  mapped_reads <- ggplot(input_counts)+
  geom_col(aes(x=Label, y=Mapped, fill=CellLine, alpha='Mapped Reads'))+
  geom_col(aes(x=Label, y=Reads, fill=CellLine, alpha='Total Reads'))+
  scale_fill_brewer(type='qual',palette='Set2')+
  scale_alpha_manual(values=c(1,0.5))+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=input_counts$TotalsgRNAs[[1]] * 300,linetype='300x Coverage Threshold'),col='red',)+
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c('dashed'))+
  geom_text(aes(x=Label, y=Mapped, label=format(round(Percentage, digits=1), nsmall = 1) ),size = 3.5, vjust = -0.5)+
  labs(x='Timepoint', y='Number of Reads')+ 
       # title=str_wrap('Mapping QC indicates good quality of raw reads with mapping ratio > 65% and majority of samples with >300x read coverage', width=55),
       #  #subtitle='Numbers indicate % of mapped reads and R indicates replicate number',
       # caption=substitute(paste('Threshold as per (Li ',italic('et al,'), ' 2015)')))+
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=60, vjust = +0.5),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust = -0.15))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(#'Plasmid',
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)','Day 21 (R1)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)','Day 21 (R2)',
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)','Day 21 (R1)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)','Day 21 (R2)'))

return(mapped_reads)
}
  
dipg_mapping_QC <- mapping_ratio_QC(count_summary, plasmid=FALSE)
print(dipg_mapping_QC)
```

```{r read QC - zerocounts}

zerocount_QC <- function(input_counts, plasmid){
  
    if (plasmid == FALSE){
    input_counts <- filter(input_counts,Label != 'Plasmid')
  }
  
  zerocounts <- ggplot(input_counts, aes(x=Label, y=Zerocounts))+
    geom_col(aes(fill=CellLine))+
    scale_fill_brewer(type='qual',palette='Set2')+
    labs(x='Timepoint', y='Number of gRNAs with zero mapped reads')+
       # title=str_wrap('Low number of sgRNAs with zero reads supports good quality of raw reads', width = 55),
       # caption=substitute(paste('Threshold as per (Li ',italic('et al,'), ' 2015)')))+
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept=input_counts$TotalsgRNAs[[1]] * 0.01,linetype='1% of Total gRNAs Threshold'),col='red')+
    scale_linetype_manual("Title", values = 2)+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=60, vjust=+0.5),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust = -0.15))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(#'Plasmid',
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)','Day 21 (R1)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)','Day 21 (R2)',
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)','Day 21 (R1)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)','Day 21 (R2)'))
  
  return(zerocounts)
  
}

dipg_zerocount <- zerocount_QC(count_summary, plasmid=FALSE)
print(dipg_zerocount)

```

```{r read QC - Gini}

giniindex_QC <- function(input_counts, plasmid){
    if (plasmid == FALSE){
    input_counts <- filter(input_counts,Label != 'Plasmid')
  }
  
  gini_index <-ggplot(input_counts, aes(x=Label, y=GiniIndex))+
    geom_col(aes(fill=CellLine))+
    scale_fill_brewer(type='qual',palette='Set2')+
    labs(x='Timepoint', y='Gini Index')+ 
       # title=str_wrap('Low Gini Index indicates even distribution of raw reads across samples and supports good quality of reads', width = 55),
       # caption=substitute(paste('Threshold as per (Li ',italic('et al,'), ' 2015)')))+
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0.1,linetype='Threshold for Baseline Samples'),col='red')+
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0.2,linetype='Threshold for Endpoint Samples'),col='black')+
    scale_linetype_manual(name = "Threshold", values = c(2, 2), 
                          guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = c('red','black'))))+
    scale_colour_manual(values=c('red','black'))+ 
    theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=60, vjust = +0.5),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust = -0.15))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(#'Plasmid',
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)','Day 21 (R1)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)','Day 21 (R2)',
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)','Day 21 (R1)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)','Day 21 (R2)'))
  
  return(gini_index)
}

dipg_giniindex <- giniindex_QC(count_summary, plasmid=FALSE)
print(dipg_giniindex)
```

```{r read_distribution}

read_distribution_plotter <- function(input_counts, samples_to_include,title='', nt_controls = FALSE){
  
  if (nt_controls == TRUE){
    input_counts <- filter(input_counts, is.na(Gene))
  }
  
  melted_counts <- input_counts %>%
    select(sgRNA,Gene,samples_to_include) %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(sample = variable, count = value) %>%
    mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(sample,'CON')~'K27M',
                                str_detect(sample,'KO')~'K27M-KO',
                                str_detect(sample,'Plasmid')~'Plasmid')) %>%
    mutate(Timepoint = case_when(str_detect(sample,'(d0)|(Plasmid)')~'0',
                                 str_detect(sample,'d11')~'11',
                                 str_detect(sample,'d21')~'21',
                                 ))
  
  read_distribution <- ggplot(melted_counts) +
    geom_density(aes(x=count, col=CellLine, linetype=Timepoint)) +
    scale_colour_brewer(name='Cell Line',type='qual',palette='Set2')+
    scale_linetype_manual(name= 'Timepoint (Day)',labels=c('0','11','21'), values = c('solid','longdash','dotted'))+
    labs(x='Read Count', y='Density', 
       title=str_wrap(title, width=65))
  
  return(read_distribution)
}

# dipg_read_distribution_rep1 <- read_distribution_plotter(raw_counts,rep1_samples,'Distribution of raw read counts for replicate 1 samples')
# dipg_read_distribution_rep2 <- read_distribution_plotter(raw_counts,rep2_samples,'Distribution of raw read counts for replicate 2 samples')

# print(dipg_read_distribution_rep2)

# print(dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1)
# print(dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2)
#

# print(dipg_read_distribution_rep1_norm)
# print(dipg_read_distribution_rep2_norm)
# 
# dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1_norm <- read_distribution_plotter(norm_counts,rep1_samples, 'Distribution of normalised read counts of non-targeting gRNAs for replicate 1 samples',nt_controls = TRUE)
# dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2_norm <- read_distribution_plotter(norm_counts,rep2_samples, 'Distribution of normalised read counts of non-targeting gRNAs for replicate 2 samples',nt_controls = TRUE)
# print(dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1_norm)
# print(dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2_norm)

```

```{r read_distribution_gRNAs}

gRNAs_distribution_plotter <- function(input_counts, samples_to_include, nt_controls = FALSE){
  
  if (nt_controls == TRUE){
    input_counts <- filter(input_counts, is.na(Gene))
  }
  
  data_list <- vector('list', length(samples_to_include))
  
  for (i in seq_along(samples_to_include)){
    data_list[[i]]  <- input_counts %>%
    select(sgRNA,Gene,samples_to_include[i]) %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(sample = variable, count = value) %>%
    group_by(Gene, sample)%>%
    summarise(Gene,
              sample,
              count = mean(count)) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    #ungroup() %>%
    #group_by(sample)%>%
    #filter(rank(unique(Gene), ties.method='min') %% 100 == 0) %>% #Thins down the number of genes plotted/analysed to a subset of 20k genes
    mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(sample,'CON')~'K27M',
                                str_detect(sample,'KO')~'K27M-KO',
                                str_detect(sample,'Plasmid')~'Plasmid')) %>%
    mutate(CellLine = factor(CellLine, levels=c('Plasmid','K27M','K27M-KO'), ordered=TRUE)) %>%
    mutate(Timepoint = case_when(str_detect(sample,'(d0)|(Plasmid)')~'0',
                                 str_detect(sample,'d11')~'11',
                                 str_detect(sample,'d21')~'21',
                                 )) %>%
    mutate(Replicate = case_when(str_detect(sample,'r1')~'1',
                                 str_detect(sample,'r2')~'2',
                                 TRUE ~ '1')) %>%
    ungroup()
  }
  
  if (str_detect(samples_to_include[2],'CON')){
    colour_labels <- c('Plasmid','K27M')
    colour_values <- c('#66c2a5','#fc8d62')
  } else if(str_detect(samples_to_include[2], 'KO')){
    colour_labels <- c('Plasmid','K27M-KO')
    colour_values <- c('#66c2a5','#8da0cb')
  }

  read_distribution <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = data_list[[1]],aes(x=as.numeric(reorder(Gene, -count)), y=count,col=CellLine, linetype=Timepoint)) +
    geom_line(data = data_list[[2]],aes(x=as.numeric(reorder(Gene, -count)), y=count,col=CellLine, linetype=Timepoint)) +
    geom_line(data = data_list[[3]],aes(x=as.numeric(reorder(Gene, -count)), y=count,col=CellLine, linetype=Timepoint)) +
    geom_line(data = data_list[[4]],aes(x=as.numeric(reorder(Gene, -count)), y=count,col=CellLine, linetype=Timepoint)) +
    labs(x='Genes', y='Read Count', 
       title=str_wrap('Distribution of read counts over all genes for replicate 1 samples indicates similar shape over time', width = 65))+
    scale_colour_manual(labels=colour_labels, values=colour_values)+
    scale_linetype_manual(labels = c('Baseline', 'Midpoint', 'Endpoint'), values=c('solid', 'dashed', 'dotted'))+
    theme(axis.text.x =element_blank(),
          axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
          legend.title=element_blank())
  
  print(read_distribution)
  
  return(read_distribution)
}
# combined_CON_gRNAs_distribution <- gRNAs_distribution_plotter(norm_counts,
#                                                           c('Plasmid','CON_r1_d0','CON_r1_d11','CON_r1_d21'))
# combined_KO_gRNAs_distribution <- gRNAs_distribution_plotter(norm_counts,
#                                                              c('Plasmid','KO_r1_d0','KO_r1_d11','KO_r1_d21'))
                                                            
```


```{r read QC-export, include=FALSE}
read_QC_plots <- list(dipg_mapping_QC,dipg_zerocount,dipg_giniindex) 

# dipg_read_distribution_rep1,dipg_read_distribution_rep2, dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1,dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2,dipg_read_distribution_rep1_norm,dipg_read_distribution_rep2_norm, dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1_norm,dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2_norm,combined_CON_gRNAs_distribution, combined_KO_gRNAs_distribution)
multiple_plot_exporter(read_QC_plots, 'dipg_read_qc')

rm(dipg_mapping_QC,dipg_zerocount,dipg_giniindex)
# dipg_read_distribution_rep1,dipg_read_distribution_rep2, dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1,dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2,dipg_read_distribution_rep1_norm,dipg_read_distribution_rep2_norm, dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep1_norm,dipg_nt_control_read_distribution_rep2_norm, combined_CON_gRNAs_distribution, combined_KO_gRNAs_distribution)

rm(read_QC_plots)
```


```{r outlier_thresholding}
#!Converts values to NA if read > threshold and then converts value of NA to mean for each column
# Make sure you really need this before you use this code because it uses an arbitrary threshold to define outliers. It is advised to use the outlier thresholding option in MAGeCK.

norm_counts <- norm_counts %>% 
  expss::na_if(expss::gt(3000))

for (i in seq(3,ncol(norm_counts))){
  norm_counts[[i]] <- replace_na(norm_counts[[i]], mean(norm_counts[[i]], na.rm=TRUE))
}

#write_tsv(norm_counts,'count1b.count.normalized_thresholded.txt')

```

## Quality Control: Replicate Level

Such QC aims to check for consistency between biological replicates and involves:

- Distribution of normalised read counts via boxplots; cumulative distribution functions
  - Expected that replicates have similar distributions
- Pairwise Pearson correlation of log(read counts) between samples
  - Expected > 0.8 between bioreplicates and < 0.8 between non bioreplicate samples
- Principal component analysis (for dimensionality reduction) with visualisation on first 3x pairs of PCs
  - Expected that bioreplicate samples cluster together
  
```{r replicate_level_qc}
#!Need to define the groups of bioreplicates and the name for each group
biorep_groups <- list(
  c('Plasmid'),
  c('CON_r1_d0','CON_r2_d0'),
  c('CON_r1_d11','CON_r2_d11'),
  c('CON_r1_d21','CON_r2_d21'),
  c('KO_r1_d0','KO_r2_d0'),
  c('KO_r1_d11','KO_r2_d11'),
  c('KO_r1_d21','KO_r2_d21')
  )
biorep_groupnames <- c(
  'Plasmid',
  'Baseline K27M',
  'Midpoint K27M',
  'Endpoint K27M',
  'Baseline K27M-KO',
  'Midpoint K27M-KO',
  'Endpoint K27M-KO'
)

biorep_annotator <- function(input_norm_counts, samples_to_include, biorep_group_names){ #Ensure that samples to include is a list with character vectors containing names of bioreplicates
  
  all_samples <- flatten_chr(samples_to_include)
  
  norm_counts <- input_norm_counts %>%
    select(Gene, sgRNA, contains(all_of(all_samples))) %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(sample=  variable, norm_count = value)
    
  
  #Max seven biorep groups (add more if needed) corresponding to baseline; midpoint; endpoint
  if (length(samples_to_include) == 1){
    norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
       mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
        sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 1
        ))
  } else if (length(samples_to_include) == 2){
      norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
        mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 1,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[2]] ~ 2
           ))
  } else if (length(samples_to_include) == 3){
      norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
        mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 1,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[2]] ~ 2,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[3]] ~ 3
         ))
      
  } else if (length(samples_to_include) == 4){
      norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
        mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 1,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[2]] ~ 2,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[3]] ~ 3,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[4]] ~ 4
         ))
      
  } else if (length(samples_to_include) == 5){
      norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
        mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 1,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[2]] ~ 2,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[3]] ~ 3,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[4]] ~ 4,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[5]] ~ 5
         ))
      
  } else if (length(samples_to_include) == 6){
      norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
        mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 1,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[2]] ~ 2,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[3]] ~ 3,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[4]] ~ 4,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[5]] ~ 5,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[6]] ~ 6
         ))
  }  else if (length(samples_to_include) == 7){ #If plasmid included
      norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
        mutate(biorep_group = case_when(
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[1]] ~ 0,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[2]] ~ 1,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[3]] ~ 2,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[4]] ~ 3,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[5]] ~ 4,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[6]] ~ 5,
            sample %in% samples_to_include[[7]] ~ 6
         ))
  }else {
    print('You need to alter function to accept more biorep groups')
  }
  
  norm_counts$biorep_group <- factor(norm_counts$biorep_group, seq(0,length(samples_to_include)))
  
  norm_counts <-mutate(norm_counts, Replicate = case_when(str_detect(sample,'r1')~'1',
                                 str_detect(sample,'r2')~'2',
                                 TRUE ~ '0')) #Adds 0 for Plasmid 
  
  return(norm_counts)
  
}
  
biorep_norm_read_dist_plotter <- function(norm_counts, samples_to_include, biorep_group_names, prefix){ #Ensure that samples to include is a list with character vectors containing names of bioreplicates
  
  #Filter out plasmid
  norm_counts <- norm_counts %>%
    filter(!str_detect(sample,'Plasmid'))
  
  biorep_freqpoly_distribution <- ggplot(norm_counts, aes(x=norm_count))+
    geom_freqpoly(aes(col=biorep_group,linetype = Replicate))+
    scale_colour_brewer(name='Bioreplicate Group', type='qual',palette='Set2', labels=biorep_group_names)+
    scale_linetype_manual(name = 'Replicate', values = c('solid','dotted'))+
    xlab('Counts per gRNA')+
    ylab('Frequency')+
    labs(title=str_wrap('Bioreplicates have similar frequency distributions of normalised read count', width = 65))+
    theme(legend.text = element_text(),
          text = element_text(size = 16))+
    scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,2000))
  
  print(biorep_freqpoly_distribution)
  #ggsave(paste(prefix,'biorep_freqpoly_distribution.png',sep='_'), biorep_freqpoly_distribution)
  
  biorep_boxplot_distribution <- ggplot(norm_counts, aes(x=sample, y=norm_count))+
    geom_boxplot(aes(fill=biorep_group))+
    geom_signif(comparisons=samples_to_include, test='wilcox.test', map_signif_level = TRUE)+
    scale_fill_brewer(name='Bioreplicate Group', type='qual',palette='Set2', labels=biorep_group_names)+
    xlab('Replicate Number')+
    ylab('Counts per gRNA')+
    labs(title=str_wrap('Boxplot of normalised read counts shows visually similar but statistically dissimilar reads between bioreplicates', width = 65),
         caption = 'Statistical test via Wilcoxon Rank Sum test')+
    theme(legend.text = element_text(),
                       text = element_text(size = 16))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = rep(c('1','2'),6))
                              
   
  
  print(biorep_boxplot_distribution)
  #ggsave(paste(prefix,'biorep_boxplot_distribution.png',sep='_'), biorep_boxplot_distribution)
  
  return(list(biorep_freqpoly_distribution, biorep_boxplot_distribution))
    
}

pairwise_correlation_plotter <- function(norm_counts, samples_to_include, biorep_group_names, prefix){
  
#!Filter out plasmid
  norm_counts <- select(norm_counts,1:14)
  
  cor_matrix <- norm_counts %>%
    select(flatten_chr(samples_to_include)) %>%
    log10() %>%
    filter(CON_r1_d11 != -Inf,
           KO_r2_d21 != -Inf)
    
  
  cor_values <- cor(cor_matrix, use='complete.obs')
  
  #Reorders the correlation matrix via hierarchial clustering to group similar correlation coefficients together
  #cor_distance <- as.dist((1-cor_values)/2)
  #hier_cluster <- hclust(cor_distance)
  #cor_values <- cor_values[hier_cluster$order, hier_cluster$order]
  
  cor_values[upper.tri(cor_values)] <- NA #Only taking one half of the correlation matrix given its reflection
  cor_values <- melt(cor_values, na.rm=TRUE) %>%
    mutate(Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r1_d0') & str_detect(Var1, 'CON_r2_d0')~'biorep',
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r1_d11') & str_detect(Var1, 'CON_r2_d11')~'biorep',
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r1_d21') & str_detect(Var1, 'CON_r2_d21')~'biorep',
      str_detect(Var2, 'KO_r1_d0') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r2_d0')~'biorep',
      str_detect(Var2, 'KO_r1_d11') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r2_d11')~'biorep',
      str_detect(Var2, 'KO_r1_d21') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r2_d21')~'biorep',
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r1_d0') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r1_d0')~'baseline',
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r1_d0') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r2_d0')~'baseline',
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r2_d0') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r1_d0')~'baseline',
      str_detect(Var2, 'CON_r2_d0') & str_detect(Var1, 'KO_r2_d0')~'baseline',

      TRUE ~ 'neither'
    )) %>%
    mutate(Group = factor(Group, levels=c('neither','biorep','baseline'), ordered=TRUE))
  
  pairwise_cor_plot <- ggplot(cor_values, aes(Var1, Var2, fill=value))+
    geom_tile(aes(col=Group),lwd=0.5, show.legend = FALSE)+
    scale_colour_manual(values=c('white','red', 'purple'))+
    scale_fill_viridis(limits=c(0,1), name='Pearson Correlation')+
    labs(title=str_wrap('Pairwise Pearson correlation coefficients between samples show strong correlation between bioreplicates', width=65),
         #subtitle=str_wrap('Red-lined tiles show correlation between bioreplicates and within Day 0 samples',width=65)
         ) +
    geom_text(aes(label=round(value,3)), colour = 'black')+
    theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
          axis.title.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks = element_blank(),
          legend.direction='horizontal',
          axis.text.x=element_text(angle=90),
                       text = element_text(size = 16))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c('K27M Day 0 (R1)','K27M Day 0 (R2)',
                              'K27M Day 11 (R1)','K27M Day 11 (R2)',
                              'K27M Day 21 (R1)','K27M Day 21 (R2)',
                              'KO Day 0 (R1)','KO Day 0 (R2)',
                              'KO Day 11 (R1)','KO Day 11 (R2)',
                              'KO Day 21 (R1)','KO Day 21 (R2)'))+
    scale_y_discrete(labels = c('K27M Day 0 (R1)','K27M Day 0 (R2)',
                              'K27M Day 11 (R1)','K27M Day 11 (R2)',
                              'K27M Day 21 (R1)','K27M Day 21 (R2)',
                              'KO Day 0 (R1)','KO Day 0 (R2)',
                              'KO Day 11 (R1)','KO Day 11 (R2)',
                              'KO Day 21 (R1)','KO Day 21 (R2)'))
    
    guides(fill = guide_colourbar(barheight = 1, barwidth = 5, title.position='top', title.hjust=0.5))
  
    #ggsave(paste(prefix,'pairwise_correlation_plot.png', sep='_'), pairwise_cor_plot)
  
  print(pairwise_cor_plot)
  return(pairwise_cor_plot)
}


biorep_norm_counts <- biorep_annotator(norm_counts, biorep_groups, biorep_groupnames) #Melts the normalised read count table and adds a biorep group column

biorep_norm_plots <- biorep_norm_read_dist_plotter(biorep_norm_counts, biorep_groups[2:7], biorep_groupnames[2:7], prefix='dipg')
biorep_correlation_matrix <- pairwise_correlation_plotter(norm_counts, biorep_groups[2:7], biorep_groupnames[2,7], prefix='dipg')
```

```{r biorep_pca}
row_biorep_group <- sort(c(0,seq(1,6),seq(1,6))) #0 refers to the plasmid
row_biorep_replicate_number <- c(0,rep(seq(1,2),6)) #0 refers to the plasmid

biorep_pca_output <- function(input_norm_counts){
  
    num_na_values <- sum(is.na(input_norm_counts$norm_count))
    print(paste('Number of NA values to be removed from biorep_norm_counts is', as.character(num_na_values)))
  
    pre_transposed_norm_counts <<- input_norm_counts %>%
    drop_na(norm_count) %>% #Filter out NA values
    select(sgRNA, sample, norm_count)
      
    transposed_norm_counts <<- pre_transposed_norm_counts %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = sgRNA, values_from = norm_count, values_fill=mean(pre_transposed_norm_counts$norm_count, na.rm=TRUE)) %>% #Replace NA outlier values with mean count value
      column_to_rownames(var='sample')
    
    row_samplenames <<- rownames(transposed_norm_counts) 
    pca_bioreps <- prcomp(transposed_norm_counts, scale=TRUE)
    
    return(pca_bioreps)
}


biorep_pca_scree_plotter <- function(pca_bioreps){
  
  pca_var_explained <<- pca_bioreps$sdev^2/sum(pca_bioreps$sdev^2)
  
  scree_tb <- tibble('PC' = as.factor(seq(1,length(pca_var_explained))),
                     'variance' = pca_var_explained)
  
  scree_plot <- ggplot(scree_tb, aes(PC, variance * 100))+
    geom_col()+
    labs(x='Principal Component', y='Percentage of Variance Explained',
         title=str_wrap('Scree plot of principal component analysis of all samples shows explanation of majority of variance by first three principal components', width = 65))+
    theme(text = element_text(size = 16))
  
  print(scree_plot)
  
  return(scree_plot)
}

pca_pairwise_plotter <- function(pca, num_pcs_interest){
  
  pc <- as_tibble(pca$x) %>%
    select(1:num_pcs_interest)
  
  combinations <- combn(num_pcs_interest,2)
  
  pairwise_plots <- vector('list', ncol(combinations))
  
  for (i in seq(1,ncol(combinations))){
    subsetted_pc <- select(pc,combinations[,i]) %>%
      cbind(row_samplenames) %>%
      cbind(row_biorep_group)%>%
      cbind(row_biorep_replicate_number)
    
    pairwise_plots[[i]] <- ggplot(subsetted_pc,aes(subsetted_pc[[1]],subsetted_pc[[2]]))+
      geom_point(aes(col=as.factor(row_biorep_group)))+
      #geom_text_repel(aes(label=row_biorep_replicate_n))+
      xlab(paste(colnames(subsetted_pc)[[1]],': ',
                 as.character(round(pca_var_explained[combinations[1,i]]*100)),'% variance explained',sep=''))+
      ylab(paste(colnames(subsetted_pc)[[2]],': ',
                 as.character(round(pca_var_explained[combinations[2,i]]*100)),'% variance explained',sep=''))+
      scale_colour_brewer(name='Bioreplicate Group', labels=biorep_groupnames, type = 'qual', palette='Set2')+
      labs(title=str_wrap(paste('Biplot of principal components', colnames(subsetted_pc)[[1]], 'and', colnames(subsetted_pc)[[2]],'shows variable clustering of replicates'), width = 65))+
      theme(text = element_text(size = 16))
    
    print(pairwise_plots[[i]])
    
    pdf(paste('pca_pairwise_plot',as.character(i),'.pdf',sep=''))
    print(pairwise_plots[[i]])
    dev.off()
    
  }

}

biorep_pca <- biorep_pca_output(biorep_norm_counts)
biorep_scree <- biorep_pca_scree_plotter(biorep_pca)
pca_pairwise_plotter(biorep_pca,3)
```


```{r replicate_level_qc_export}
biorep_qc_plots <- list(biorep_norm_plots[[1]], biorep_norm_plots[[2]], biorep_correlation_matrix, biorep_scree #biorep_pairwise_plots[[1]], biorep_pairwise_plots[[2]], biorep_pairwise_plots[[3]]
                        )

multiple_plot_exporter(biorep_qc_plots, 'biorep_qc_plots')

rm(biorep_qc_plots)
rm(biorep_norm_plots, biorep_correlation_matrix,biorep_scree, biorep_pairwise_plots)

```
  

## Quality Control: Gene Level

Such QC can be provided via analysis of the counts for the non-targeting control gRNAs in the non-normalised count.txt across the different samples to ensure sufficiently even distribution from both baseline to endpoint samples. This provides a negative control.

The corollary positive control looks at the counts and beta-scores for the known essential genes (from DepMap) and from functional enrichment analyses (implicating such genes in ribosomal; spliceosomal function) to check that such genes are depleted from the baseline to endpoint samples.

```{r qc_gene_level}

nt_control_distribution_plotter <- function(input_norm_counts, ctrlname, samplename, samples_to_include, baseline, midpt, endpt){
  
ctrl_baseline_r1 <- paste(ctrlname,'r1',baseline,sep='_')
  ctrl_midpt_r1 <-paste(ctrlname,'r1',midpt,sep="_")
  ctrl_endpt_r1 <- paste(ctrlname,'r1',endpt,sep='_')
  sample_baseline_r1 <- paste(samplename,'r1',baseline,sep='_')
  sample_midpt_r1 <-paste(samplename,'r1',midpt,sep="_")
  sample_endpt_r1 <- paste(samplename,'r1',endpt,sep='_')
  
  ctrl_baseline_r2 <- paste(ctrlname,'r2',baseline,sep='_')
  ctrl_midpt_r2 <-paste(ctrlname,'r2',midpt,sep="_")
  ctrl_endpt_r2 <- paste(ctrlname,'r2',endpt,sep='_')
  sample_baseline_r2 <- paste(samplename,'r2',baseline,sep='_')
  sample_midpt_r2 <-paste(samplename,'r2',midpt,sep="_")
  sample_endpt_r2 <- paste(samplename,'r2',endpt,sep='_')
  
  nt_control_counts <- input_norm_counts %>%
    filter(is.na(Gene)) %>%
    select(samples_to_include) %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(sample = variable, count = value) %>%
    mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(sample,ctrlname)~'K27M', str_detect(sample,samplename)~'K27M-KO'))
  
  read_distribution <- ggplot(nt_control_counts, aes(x=sample, y=count)) +
    geom_boxplot(aes(fill=CellLine), alpha=0.75) +
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r1, ctrl_endpt_r1),c(sample_baseline_r1, sample_endpt_r1))
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='two.sided'), map_signif_level = TRUE, margin_top = 0.2)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r1,ctrl_midpt_r1),
                       c(ctrl_midpt_r1,ctrl_endpt_r1),
                       c(sample_baseline_r1,sample_midpt_r1),
                       c(sample_midpt_r1,sample_endpt_r1)
                       )
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='two.sided'), map_signif_level = TRUE)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r2, ctrl_endpt_r2),c(sample_baseline_r2, sample_endpt_r2))
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='two.sided'), map_signif_level = TRUE, margin_top = 0.2)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r2,ctrl_midpt_r2),
                       c(ctrl_midpt_r2,ctrl_endpt_r2),
                       c(sample_baseline_r2,sample_midpt_r2),
                       c(sample_midpt_r2,sample_endpt_r2)
                       )
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='two.sided'), map_signif_level = TRUE)+
    scale_fill_brewer(type='qual',palette='Set2')+
    labs(x='Timepoint', y='Counts per gRNA', 
       title=str_wrap('Normalised read counts for non-targeting controls are similar from baseline to endpoint samples', width =65),
       caption='Statistical test via two-sided Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test'
       #subtitle='R refers to bioreplicate number'
       )+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=60, vjust = +0.5))+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c('Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)', 'Day 21 (R1)' ,
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)', 'Day 21 (R1)' ,
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)', 'Day 21 (R2)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)', 'Day 21 (R2)'))
    
  print(read_distribution)
  return(read_distribution)
  
  
}

positive_control_count_plotter <- function(input_normalised_counts,ctrlname, samplename, samples_to_include, baseline, midpt, endpt, depmap_or_tsg){
  
  ctrl_baseline_r1 <- paste(ctrlname,'r1',baseline,sep='_')
  ctrl_midpt_r1 <-paste(ctrlname,'r1',midpt,sep="_")
  ctrl_endpt_r1 <- paste(ctrlname,'r1',endpt,sep='_')
  sample_baseline_r1 <- paste(samplename,'r1',baseline,sep='_')
  sample_midpt_r1 <-paste(samplename,'r1',midpt,sep="_")
  sample_endpt_r1 <- paste(samplename,'r1',endpt,sep='_')
  
  ctrl_baseline_r2 <- paste(ctrlname,'r2',baseline,sep='_')
  ctrl_midpt_r2 <-paste(ctrlname,'r2',midpt,sep="_")
  ctrl_endpt_r2 <- paste(ctrlname,'r2',endpt,sep='_')
  sample_baseline_r2 <- paste(samplename,'r2',baseline,sep='_')
  sample_midpt_r2 <-paste(samplename,'r2',midpt,sep="_")
  sample_endpt_r2 <- paste(samplename,'r2',endpt,sep='_')
  
  if (depmap_or_tsg == 'depmap'){
    plot_counts <- input_normalised_counts %>%
      filter(Gene %in% common_essentials$gene)
  } else if (depmap_or_tsg == 'tsg'){
        plot_counts <- input_normalised_counts %>%
      filter(Gene %in% tsgenes$gene)
  }
  plot_counts <- plot_counts %>%
    select(samples_to_include) %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(sample=variable, count=value) %>%
    mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(sample,ctrlname)~'K27M', str_detect(sample,samplename)~'K27M-KO'))
  
  
  read_distribution <- ggplot(plot_counts,aes(x=sample, y=count)) +
    geom_boxplot(aes(fill=CellLine), alpha=0.75) +
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r1, ctrl_endpt_r1),c(sample_baseline_r1, sample_endpt_r1))
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='greater'), map_signif_level = TRUE, margin_top = 0.2)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r1,ctrl_midpt_r1),
                       c(ctrl_midpt_r1,ctrl_endpt_r1),
                       c(sample_baseline_r1,sample_midpt_r1),
                       c(sample_midpt_r1,sample_endpt_r1)
                       )
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='greater'), map_signif_level = TRUE)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r2, ctrl_endpt_r2),c(sample_baseline_r2, sample_endpt_r2))
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='greater'), map_signif_level = TRUE, margin_top = 0.2)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=
                  list(c(ctrl_baseline_r2,ctrl_midpt_r2),
                       c(ctrl_midpt_r2,ctrl_endpt_r2),
                       c(sample_baseline_r2,sample_midpt_r2),
                       c(sample_midpt_r2,sample_endpt_r2)
                       )
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='greater'), map_signif_level = TRUE)+
    scale_fill_brewer(type='qual',palette='Set2')+
    labs(x='Timepoint', y='Counts per gRNA', 
       title=str_wrap('Normalised read counts of DepMap common essential genes decrease significantly from baseline to endpoint samples', width =65),
       caption='Statistical test via one-sided Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test'
       #subtitle='R refers to bioreplicate number'
       )+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=60, vjust = +0.5))+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c('Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)', 'Day 21 (R1)' ,
                              'Day 0 (R1)','Day 11 (R1)', 'Day 21 (R1)' ,
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)', 'Day 21 (R2)',
                              'Day 0 (R2)','Day 11 (R2)', 'Day 21 (R2)'))
  
  print(read_distribution)
  
  return(read_distribution)
}

dipg_nt_control_counts<- nt_control_distribution_plotter(norm_counts, 'CON','KO',c(rep1_samples,rep2_samples),'d0','d11','d21')
dipg_depmap_control_counts <- positive_control_count_plotter(norm_counts, 'CON','KO',c(rep1_samples,rep2_samples),'d0','d11','d21', 'depmap')
dipg_tsg_control_counts <- positive_control_count_plotter(norm_counts, 'CON','KO',c(rep1_samples,rep2_samples),'d0','d11','d21', 'tsg')


dipg_control_count_plots <- list(dipg_nt_control_counts, dipg_depmap_control_counts, dipg_tsg_control_counts)
multiple_plot_exporter(dipg_control_count_plots, 'dipg_control_counts')

rm(dipg_nt_control_counts, dipg_depmap_control_counts)
rm(dipg_control_count_plots)

```

```{r read_count_over_time}
# counts_boxplot_plotter <- function(input_raw_counts, ctrlname, samplename, samples_to_include, baseline, midpt, endpt){
#   
#   ctrl_baseline <- paste(ctrlname,baseline,sep='_')
#   ctrl_midpt <- paste(ctrlname,midpt,sep="_")
#   ctrl_endpt <- paste(ctrlname,endpt,sep='_')
#   sample_baseline <- paste(samplename,baseline,sep='_')
#   sample_midpt <-paste(samplename,midpt,sep="_")
#   sample_endpt <- paste(samplename,endpt,sep='_')

#     select(samples_to_include) %>%
#     reshape2::melt() %>%

#     mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(sample,ctrlname)~ctrlname, str_detect(sample,samplename)~samplename))

#   read_distribution <- ggplot(counts, aes(x=sample, y=count)) +
#     geom_boxplot(aes(fill=CellLine), alpha=0.75) +
#     geom_signif(comparisons=
#                   list(c(ctrl_baseline, ctrl_endpt),c(sample_baseline, sample_endpt))
#                   ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='two.sided'), map_signif_level = TRUE, margin_top = 0.2)+
#     geom_signif(comparisons=
#                   list(c(ctrl_baseline,ctrl_midpt),
#                        c(ctrl_midpt,ctrl_endpt),
#                        c(sample_baseline,sample_midpt),
#                        c(sample_midpt,sample_endpt)
#                        )
#                   ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative='two.sided'), map_signif_level = TRUE)+
#     scale_fill_brewer(type='qual',palette='Set2')+
```
