```{r read_count_over_time}
#        title=str_wrap(paste('Read counts for all genes over all samples of bioreplicate',substr(baseline,2,2)), width =75),
#        caption='Statistical test via two-sided Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test')+
#     theme(legend.title = element_blank())
#   
#   return(read_distribution)
# }
# 
# counts_boxplot_rep1 <- counts_boxplot_plotter(norm_counts, 'CON','KO',rep1_samples,'r1_d0','r1_d11','r1_d21')
# counts_boxplot_rep2 <- counts_boxplot_plotter(norm_counts, 'CON','KO',rep2_samples,'r2_d0','r2_d11','r2_d21')
# 
# print(counts_boxplot_rep1)
# print(counts_boxplot_rep2)
# 
# multiple_plot_exporter(list(counts_boxplot_rep1,counts_boxplot_rep2), 'counts_replicates_boxplots')
# rm(counts_boxplot_rep1, counts_boxplot_rep2)
# 

```


## Downstream Functional Analysis with MAGeCKFlute

This uses functions of the *MAGeCKFlute* package @Wang2019 to run downstream QC and functional analysis of the results from MAGeCK MLE.
For reference, the beta score from MAGeCK-MLE provides a measure of selection (+ve scores reflect positive selection and vice versa).

```{r import_mle}
#! Need to define your MAGeCK mle derived gene_summary.txt here
mle_gene_summary <- gene_summary_importer('mle2b.gene_summary.txt',working_directory)
#mle_dipg_essential <- gene_summary_importer('dipg_essential_v2.gene_summary.txt',working_directory)

#! This is not required if you don't want to change the column names
column_names <-colnames(mle_gene_summary)
column_names <- str_replace(column_names,'CON3','CON') 
column_names <- str_replace(column_names,'KO2','KO')
colnames(mle_gene_summary) <- column_names

#! Need to define your MAGeCK mle derived gene_summary.txt here
mle_betas <- ReadBeta(paste(working_directory,'mle2b.gene_summary.txt', sep='/')) %>%
  #!Below code not required if you don't want to change column names
  rename(CON=CON3, KO=KO2)
```

### Beta Normalisation via Core Essential Genes 

Different cell lines and different conditions may impact the doubling times so comparing cell lines/conditions with different proliferation rates may lead to inherent biases. For example, cell lines with faster doubling times will have more generations so will have greater magnitude of selection for or against their KO mutation.

This differential proliferation rate can be accounted for by normalising the beta-values for all cell lines/conditions using a list of 625 core essential genes, assuming that they are equally negatively selected against in all lines/conditions. This is achieved by

1. Calculating the median beta-score from beta-scores for the 625 core essential genes across all conditions
2. Normalising all beta-scores for all genes across all conditions by dividing by the median calculated in 1)

This normalisation allows comparison between the different cell lines and conditions.

```{r beta_normalisation}
#!Define your control and sample condition names
ctrlname <- 'CON'
samplename <- 'KO'

#Adds column with Human equivalent gene ID though some genes will have NA values
mle_betas$HumanGene = TransGeneID(mle_betas$Gene, fromType = "symbol", toType = "symbol")

#Performs median normalisation of beta values using the median value of the core essential genes of cell cycle
#!Need to alter samples() argument to specify which conditions i.e columns in mle_betas you want to normalise for.
mle_betas_cc = NormalizeBeta(mle_betas, id = "HumanGene", samples=c(ctrlname, samplename,'d11','d21'), 
                         method="cell_cycle")
```

### Visualisation of Normalised Beta Values

After normalisation, the distributions of beta values should be similar across all cell lines/conditions. This can be assessed via:

1. Density plot for beta scores of conditions 1 and 2
2. Density plot for differences in beta scores between conditions 1 and 2
3. Comparison plot of beta scores for condition 1 vs. condition 2

N.B This code works but isn't used very much!

```{r normalisation_visualiser}
density_beta_normalised_plotter <- function(normalised_betas,ctrlname, samplename, titl,capt='',filename, prefix){
  
  melted_df <- reshape2::melt(normalised_betas) %>%
    rename(CellLine = variable, beta = value)
  
  density_beta <- ggplot(melted_df)+
    geom_density(aes(beta, col=CellLine))+
    labs(title=str_wrap(titl, width=65), caption=str_wrap(capt,width=60))+
    xlab('Beta score')+
    ylab('Density')+
    scale_colour_brewer(type='qual', palette='Set2')+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank())
    
  #ggsave(paste(prefix,filename,sep='_'), density_beta)
  
  return(density_beta)
}

density_diff_beta_normalised_plotter <- function(normalised_betas,ctrlname, samplename, titl,capt='',filename, prefix){
  
  diff_df <- normalised_betas %>%
    mutate(diff = normalised_betas[,3] - normalised_betas[,2]) %>%
    select(Gene, HumanGene, diff)
  
  melted_df <- reshape2::melt(diff_df) %>%
    rename(CellLine = variable, diff=value)
  
  density_diff_beta <- ggplot(melted_df)+
    geom_density(aes(diff))+
    labs(title=str_wrap(titl, width=65), caption=str_wrap(capt,width=60))+
    xlab('Difference in beta scores for sample vs. control')+
    ylab('Density')+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank())+
    stat_function(fun=dnorm, args=list(mean=mean(melted_df$diff),sd=sd(melted_df$diff)), col='red', linetype=2)+
    geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=2, col='black')
    
  #ggsave(paste(prefix,filename,sep='_'), density_diff_beta)
  
  return(density_diff_beta)
}
comparative_beta_plotter <- function(normalised_betas,ctrlname, samplename, titl,capt='',filename, prefix){

  control <- sym(ctrlname) #Enables passing of function arguments to ggplot
  sample <- sym(samplename)
  
  comparative_plot <- ggplot(normalised_betas,aes(x=!!control, y=!!sample))+
    geom_point(alpha=0.5, col='black')+
    labs(title=str_wrap(titl, width=65), caption=str_wrap(capt,width=60))+
    xlab(paste(ctrlname,'beta scores'))+
    ylab(paste(samplename, 'beta scores'))+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank())+
    geom_abline(slope=1, intercept=0, col='red', alpha=0.8, linetype=2)+
    geom_smooth(method='lm')
  
  #ggsave(paste(prefix,filename,sep='_'), comparative_plot)
  
  return(comparative_plot)
}

#Repeat each function for the normalised and the non-normalised data to allow comparison
density_beta_normalised <- density_beta_normalised_plotter(mle_betas_cc, 'CON','KO','Density plot of normalised beta scores for all conditions shows normalisation',filename='beta_normalised_density.png', prefix='dipg')
density_beta_non_normalised <- density_beta_normalised_plotter(mle_betas, 'CON','KO','Density plot of non-normalised beta scores for all conditions',filename='beta_non_normalised_density.png', prefix='dipg')

density_diff_beta_normalised <- density_diff_beta_normalised_plotter(mle_betas_cc, 'CON','KO','Distribution of deviation in normalised beta scores between CON vs. KO cells tends towards normal distribution',filename='beta_normalised_density_diff.png', prefix='dipg')
density_diff_beta_non_normalised <- density_diff_beta_normalised_plotter(mle_betas, 'CON','KO','Distribution of deviation in non-normalised beta scores between CON vs. KO cells',filename='beta_non_normalised_density_diff.png', prefix='dipg')

comparative_beta_normalised <- comparative_beta_plotter(mle_betas_cc,'CON','KO','Comparison plot of normalised beta scores indicates ? as trendline (blue) ? towards y=x (red)', filename='beta_normalised_comparative_plot.png', prefix='dipg')
comparative_beta_non_normalised <- comparative_beta_plotter(mle_betas,'CON','KO','Comparison plot of non-normalised beta scores ?', filename='beta_non_normalised_comparative_plot.png', prefix='dipg')

```

```{r export_normalisation_visualiser}
normalised_vs_non_normalised <- list(density_beta_normalised, density_beta_non_normalised, density_diff_beta_normalised, density_diff_beta_non_normalised, comparative_beta_normalised, comparative_beta_non_normalised)

multiple_plot_exporter(normalised_vs_non_normalised, 'normalised_vs_non_normalised_betas')

rm(normalised_vs_non_normalised,density_beta_normalised, density_beta_non_normalised, density_diff_beta_normalised, density_diff_beta_non_normalised, comparative_beta_normalised, comparative_beta_non_normalised)

```

### Volcano Plots for Hit Identification per Condition

The below code chunk is deprecated!

```{r volcano_plot_relative_cellline}
##These DON'T USE NORMALISED BETA-VALUES BTW

volcano_plotter <- function(mle_gene_summary, input_name,comp_name, permutation_or_wald = 'Wald', top = 5, main_title){
  
  sample_gene_summary <- mle_gene_summary %>%
    select(Gene, sgRNA, starts_with(as.character(input_name))) %>%
    mutate(common_essential = case_when(Gene %in% common_essentials$gene ~ TRUE, TRUE ~ FALSE))
  
  if (permutation_or_wald == 'Wald'){
    mutated_mle_gene_summary <- sample_gene_summary %>%
      select(Gene, sgRNA, contains('beta'), contains('wald'), common_essential) %>%
      rename('fdr' = paste(as.character(input_name),'wald-fdr',sep='|')) %>%
      rename('pval' = paste(as.character(input_name),'wald-p-value',sep='|')) %>%
      mutate('significant' = case_when(fdr < 0.05 ~ TRUE, fdr >= 0.05 ~ FALSE)) %>%
      rename('beta' = paste(as.character(input_name),'beta',sep='|')) %>%
      mutate('beta_rank' = rank(-abs(beta))) %>% # Rank in descending order 
      mutate('pval_rank' = rank(abs(pval))) # Rank in descending order 
    
  }
  else if (permutation_or_wald == 'Permutation'){
    mutated_mle_gene_summary <- sample_gene_summary %>%
      select(!contains('wald')) %>%
      rename('fdr' = paste(as.character(input_name),'fdr',sep='|')) %>%
      rename('pval' = paste(as.character(input_name),'p-value',sep='|')) %>%
      mutate('significant' = case_when(fdr< 0.05 ~ TRUE, fdr >= 0.05 ~ FALSE)) %>%
      rename('beta' = paste(as.character(input_name),'beta',sep='|')) %>%
      mutate('beta_rank' = rank(-abs(beta)))%>% # Rank in descending order 
      mutate('pval_rank' = rank(abs(pval))) # Rank in descending order 
    
  } else{
    print('Need to specify Permutation or Wald')
  }
  
  if (input_name == 'CON' & comp_name == 'KO'){
    title_input <- 'K27M'
    title_comp <- 'K27M-KO'
  } else if (input_name == 'KO' & comp_name == 'CON'){
    title_input <- 'K27M-KO'
    title_comp <- 'K27M'
  }

  volcano <- ggplot(mutated_mle_gene_summary, aes(x=beta, y = -log10(fdr)))+
    geom_point(aes(col=common_essential),alpha=0.25)+
    scale_x_continuous(expand=expansion(add=0.5))+
    scale_colour_manual(name = 'Common Essential?',values=c('#fc8d62','#a6d854'),labels = c('Non Common Essential','Common Essential'))+
    #scale_alpha_manual(name = 'Statistically Signficant?',values = (c(0.2,0.5)))+
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept=-log10(0.05), linetype='5% Significance Threshold'), col='red')+
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, alpha = 0.8, col='black')+
    scale_linetype_manual(name = "Threshold", values = c(2), 
                          guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = c('red'))))+
    xlab('Beta Score')+
    ylab('-Log10 (Adjusted p value)')+
    labs(title=str_wrap(paste('Volcano plot from',permutation_or_wald, 'test for the', title_input, 'relative to the', title_comp, 'cell line indicates' ,main_title),width=65),
         caption= 'Common essential genes sourced from DepMap Q1/22')+
    theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
    guides(alpha=FALSE)+ #Removes legend for alpha
    geom_label_repel(data=filter(mutated_mle_gene_summary, beta_rank <= top | pval_rank <= top), aes(col=common_essential,label = Gene), show.legend=FALSE, max.overlaps=25, force=5, force_pull=0.5
                     )+
    scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0,2,4,6,8,10,12))
  
  return(volcano)
}

# dipg_CON_wald_volcano <- volcano_plotter(mle_gene_summary,'CON','KO', main_title='substantially greater negative selection for several hits including ASCL1', top=3)
# dipg_KO_wald_volcano <- volcano_plotter(mle_gene_summary,'KO','CON', main_title='substantially greater negative selection against common essential genes',top=3)
# 
# print(dipg_CON_wald_volcano)
# print(dipg_KO_wald_volcano)

#dipg_CON_permutation_volcano <- volcano_plotter(mle_gene_summary,'CON','KO', permutation_or_wald = 'Permutation', main_title='weak selection for or against genes', top=4)
#dipg_KO_permutation_volcano <- volcano_plotter(mle_gene_summary,'KO','CON',permutation_or_wald = 'Permutation',main_title='substantial degree of selection',top=4)

#print(dipg_CON_permutation_volcano)
#print(dipg_KO_permutation_volcano)


# dipg_volcano_plots <- list(dipg_CON_wald_volcano, dipg_KO_wald_volcano
#                            #, dipg_CON_permutation_volcano, dipg_KO_permutation_volcano
#                            )
# multiple_plot_exporter(dipg_volcano_plots, 'dipg_individual_volcanos')
# 
# rm(dipg_volcano_plots,dipg_CON_wald_volcano, dipg_KO_wald_volcano, dipg_CON_permutation_volcano, dipg_KO_permutation_volcano)

```

### Permutation Test for MAGeCK

The below code chunk is deprecated (but it works if you want to run your own permutation test though I would recommend a Wald test instead)!

This statistical test is to test if there is a significant difference for each gene between its CON and KO scores.
To do this when there is no statistical approximation that would be fully justified (e.g CLT -> normal approximation), you can use permutation (i.e randomly swapping all the Gene labels across all genes to compute difference between CON and KO scores) to generate a null distribution of difference in score.
Then, you can use this null distribution to see where the observed difference for each gene lies and compute a p-value.

The process is:
1. Sample randomly from CON pool and sample randomly from KO pool -> compute difference in score
2. Repeat n times -> null distribution of difference in score for CON vs. KO
3. Calculate p-value for each gene using the same null distribution using two-sided test (i.e convert diffs to absolute values and calculate proportion of null distribution values > observed difference)
- Also need to add 1 to both numerator and denominator to avoid misestimation as per Phipson & Smyth, 2010.

```{r permutation_test}

permutation_tester <- function(input_df,n_permutations){
  
  set.seed(0)
  null_diff <- vector('numeric',n_permutations)
  
  for (i in seq(n_permutations)){
    null_diff[i] <- sample(input_df$CON,1)-sample(input_df$KO,1)
  }
  
  null_diff_tb <- tibble(Diff = null_diff)
  null_diff_pdf <- ggplot(null_diff_tb)+
    geom_density(aes(x=Diff))+
    xlab('Difference in Beta Score')+
    ylab('Density')+
    labs(title='Probability Density Function of Difference in Beta Scores')+
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype='dashed', col='red')+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3))
  
  print(null_diff_pdf)
  
  output_pvals <- input_df %>%
    select(Gene,CON,KO,common_essential) %>%
    mutate(Diff = CON - KO) %>%
    arrange(Gene)
  
  pvals <- vector('numeric', length(input_df$Gene))
  
  for (i in seq_along(pvals)){
    diff_value <- abs(output_pvals$Diff[[i]])
    pvals[i] <- (sum(abs(null_diff)>diff_value)+1) / (length(null_diff)+1) #Calculates p-value of proportion of null distribution with greater absolute value of difference in score with correction as per Phipson & Smyth 2010.
  }
  
  adjusted_pvals <- p.adjust(pvals, method='BH')
  output_pvals <- cbind(output_pvals, pvals, adjusted_pvals)
  
  return(output_pvals)
  
}

# permuted_diff_beta <- permutation_tester(mle_betas_cc, 100000)

```

### Wald Test for MAGeCK

You can compute a Wald test (or t-test) for the difference in scores using the z-value given in mle_gene_summary for CON and KO because the z-value i.e test-statistic = beta/se(beta).
So you compute the new test-statistic of difference in betas as (difference in betas) / (var(beta1) + var(beta2) - 2*covar(beta1, beta2).

The really hard part about this is getting your covariance values between beta1 and beta2. To do this, you need to find the source file for mleem.py and replace it with the included mleem.py in the folder of this script.
Then you need to run mageck mle again with your original settings but add '>>covar.txt' to the end of the mageck mle ... command in terminal so it outputs the covar to the working directory.
MAGeCK mle outputs numerous copies of covar to that text because it runs several times - take the last set of covariance values (do this by looking at the first gene and looking for the last instance of it in the file and taking all the values below that) and copy it into Excel to make a .csv for import below.

```{r wald_tester}
#! Import your covariance.csv file here
covar <- read_csv(paste(working_directory, 'MAGeCK_CON_KO_covariance.csv', sep='/'), col_names=FALSE) %>%
  rename(Gene = X1, covar = X2)

#Adding DepMap Common Essential column to data
mle_betas_cc <- mle_betas_cc %>%
  mutate(common_essential = case_when(Gene %in% common_essentials$gene ~ TRUE, TRUE ~ FALSE)) 

#This function performs a Wald test on the difference of beta scores vs. 0
wald_tester <- function(input_mle_betas, input_mle_summary, covariance){
  
  se_vals <- input_mle_summary$`CON|beta` / input_mle_summary$`CON|z`
  
  output_pvals = input_mle_betas %>%
    mutate(Diff = CON - KO)
  
  output_pvals <- cbind(output_pvals, se_vals, covariance$covar) %>%
     rename(se = se_vals, covar=`covariance$covar`) %>%
    mutate(cc_con_var = se^2 * 2.256674^2, #Computing the variance for CON and KO but with the adjustments due to different cell cycle normalisation for CON and KO beta scores
           cc_ko_var = se^2 * 1.857476^2,
           wald_z = Diff/sqrt(cc_con_var + cc_ko_var - 2*covar),
           wald_p = 2*pnorm(abs(wald_z), lower.tail=FALSE))%>%
        filter(!is.na(Gene))
  
  fdr <- p.adjust(output_pvals$wald_p, method='BH')
  output_pvals <- cbind(output_pvals, fdr)

  return(output_pvals)
}

#This function plots a volcano plot of the derived Wald values
## Change the top value to change how many genes are labelled in the plot i.e top '6' genes for example
wald_volcano_plotter <- function(wald_vals, top){
  
  wald_vals <- wald_vals %>%
    mutate(abs_diff_rank = rank(-abs(Diff)),
           fdr_rank = rank(fdr),
           Label=case_when(Gene %in% c('ASCL1','BAMBI','AHR','ARNT','TPK1','MAP2K7','KDSR','TNFAIP6') ~ Gene, T~ ''),
           Group=case_when(fdr <= 0.05 ~ 'Sig',
                           T~'NonSig')) %>%
    mutate(Group=factor(Group, levels=c('Sig','NonSig'), ordered=T))
           
           #Deprecated chunk As per Alan's request 29/08/23
    #        Label = case_when(abs_diff_rank <= top | fdr_rank <= top ~ Gene,
    #                          TRUE ~ ''),
    #        Group = case_when(common_essential == TRUE ~ 'Common Essential',
    #                          common_essential == FALSE & fdr > 0.05 ~ 'None',
    #                          common_essential == FALSE & fdr <= 0.05 & Diff < 0 ~ 'K27M-specific Essential',
    #                          common_essential == FALSE & fdr <= 0.05 & Diff > 0 ~ 'K27M-KO-specific Essential')) %>%
    # mutate(Group = factor(Group, levels =c('K27M-specific Essential','K27M-KO-specific Essential', 'Common Essential', 'None'), ordered=TRUE))
  
  #write_tsv(wald_vals,paste('wald_diff_output.tsv'))
  
  volcano <- ggplot(wald_vals, aes(x=Diff, y = -log10(fdr)))+
    geom_point(aes(col=wald_vals$Group),alpha=0.25)+
    #scale_colour_manual(values=c('#e78ac3','#ffd92f','#a6d854','#b3b3b3'), labels=c('K27M-specific\nEssential', 'K27M-KO-specific\nEssential','Common Essential','Insignificant Difference\nin Essentiality'))+ 
    scale_colour_manual(values=c('#8b0000','#b3b3b3'), labels=c('Significant','Non-Significant'))+ #As per Alan's request 29/08/23
    scale_x_continuous(expand=expansion(add=0.5))+
    #scale_alpha_manual(name = 'Statistically Signficant?',values = (c(0.2,0.5)))+
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept=-log10(0.05), linetype='5% Significance Threshold'), col='red')+
    #geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, alpha = 0.8, col='black')+ #Deprecated as per Alan's request 29/08/23
    scale_linetype_manual(name = "Threshold", values = c(2), 
                          guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = c('red'))))+
    xlab('Difference in Beta Score')+
    ylab('- log10 (Adjusted p-value)')+
    #labs(title=str_wrap(paste('Volcano plot from a Wald test of the difference in beta scores between K27M and K27M-KO lines'),width=65),
    #     caption= 'Common essential genes sourced from DepMap Q1/22')+
    #theme(legend.title=element_blank(),
    #      legend.spacing.y=unit(0.25,'cm'))+
    #guides(colour = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
    #guides(alpha=FALSE)+ #Removes legend for alpha
    theme(legend.position="none",
          text=element_text(size=14))+ #Removes all legends as per Alan's request 29/08/23
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = wald_vals$Label, col=wald_vals$Group), show.legend=FALSE, max.overlaps=25, force=5, force_pull=0.5) #Changed to text and not box As per Alan's request 29/08/23
  
  print(volcano)
  return(volcano)
}

wald_pval_hist_plotter <- function(input_pvals){
  
  stat_significant_pvals <- filter(input_pvals, fdr <= 0.05, common_essential==FALSE)
  
  pval_hist <- ggplot(stat_significant_pvals, aes(x=-log10(fdr)))+
    geom_histogram()+
    xlab(bquote(-log[10]~'(Adjusted p value)'))+
    ylab('Count')+
    labs(title=str_wrap('Histogram of FDR values for statistically significant K27M-specific and K27M-KO-specific essential hits from Wald test for significant difference in beta scores in K27M vs. K27M-KO'),width=65)+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0,16,1))
  
  print(pval_hist)
  
  return(pval_hist)
}
  
wald_pvals <- wald_tester(mle_betas_cc, mle_gene_summary, covar)
wald_diff_volcano <- wald_volcano_plotter(wald_pvals, 5) #Changed top 5 as per Alan's request 29/08/23
ggsave('wald_diff_volcano_for_alan_230829.png',wald_diff_volcano, dpi=600, height=6, width=6) #As per Alan's request 29/08/23
wald_pval_hist <- wald_pval_hist_plotter(wald_pvals)

multiple_plot_exporter(list(wald_diff_volcano,wald_pval_hist), 'wald_diff_volcano_mageck')
```


### Condition/Cell Type-Specific Hit Identification

This normalisation of beta-score is via the cell cycle genes.

Now that we have checked normalisation of beta scores between all of our conditions/cell lines, we can identify condition/cell type-specific hits - defined as those genes which have substantially different beta scores in one of the two conditions/cell lines.

Below code is depreciated because it uses the quantile normalisation approach! 

```{r condition_specific_hit_identification}

source('./ScatterView.R') #Sourcing source code function of ScatterView() and CutoffCalling() from MAGeCKFlute package

differential_hits_plotter <- function(data, x,y, top, sd_cutoff, prefix){
  
  sample <- sym(y)
  ctrl  <- sym(x)
  slope <- 1
  
  intercept = c(-CutoffCalling(data[,y]-data[,x], sd_cutoff), CutoffCalling(data[,y]-data[,x], sd_cutoff)) #Quantile normalisation to decide arbitray threshold for selection
  
  data$common_essential[data$Gene == 'TWISTNB'] <- TRUE
  
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(Group = case_when(!!sample<slope*!!ctrl+min(intercept) ~ 'Lower',
                             !!sample>slope*!!ctrl+max(intercept) ~ 'Upper',
                             TRUE~'None')) %>%
    mutate(Group = case_when(str_detect(Group, 'Lower') & common_essential == TRUE ~ 'Common Essential',
                             str_detect(Group, 'Upper') & common_essential == TRUE ~ 'Common Essential',
                             str_detect(Group, 'None') & common_essential == TRUE ~ 'Common Essential',
                             TRUE~Group)) %>%
    mutate(Group = factor(Group, levels = c('Lower','Upper','Common Essential', 'None'), ordered=TRUE)) %>%
    mutate(Diff = !!sample - !!ctrl) %>%
    mutate(Abs_Diff = abs(Diff)) %>%
    mutate(Abs_Rank = rank(-Abs_Diff)) %>%
    mutate(Abs_Label = case_when(Abs_Rank <= top ~ Gene,
                             TRUE ~ '')) %>%
    mutate(Rank = rank(-Diff)) %>% #Rank indicates if it is positive or negative selection while Abs_rank is independent of direction
    mutate(Label = case_when(Rank <= top/2 ~ Gene,
                             Rank >= nrow(data)-top/2 ~ Gene,
                             TRUE ~ '')) %>% 
    arrange(Rank)
  
  diff_betas_cc <<- data
  
    threshold_pos_y <- data$Diff[data$Rank==100]
    threshold_neg_y <- data$Diff[data$Rank == nrow(data)-100]
    
  
  differential_hits <- ggplot(data, aes(!!ctrl, !!sample))+
    geom_point(aes(col=Group), alpha=0.25)+
    scale_colour_manual(values=c('#e78ac3','#ffd92f', '#a6d854','#b3b3b3')
, labels=c('K27M-specific\nEssential', 'K27M-KO-specific\nEssential', 'Common Essential', 'Insubstantial Difference\nin Essentiality') )+
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col='black', linetype='dotted', alpha = 0.35)+
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col='black', linetype = 'dotted', alpha = 0.35)+
    geom_abline(slope =1 , intercept =max(intercept), col='black', linetype='dashed', alpha = 0.35)+
    geom_abline(slope= 1, intercept = min(intercept), col='black', linetype = 'dashed', alpha = 0.35)+
    labs(x = 'Beta Scores of K27M-KO line',
         y ='Beta Scores of K27M line',
         title = str_wrap(paste('Comparison of beta indicates differential hits in the H3K27M line compared to H3K27M-KO line'),width = 65),
         caption= 'Common essential genes sourced from DepMap Q1/22')+
    theme(legend.title=element_blank(),
          legend.spacing.y=unit(0.25,'cm'))+
    guides(colour = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
    geom_label_repel(aes(label=Label, col=Group), max.overlaps = 30, force =5, show.legend = FALSE)

  print(differential_hits)
  
  rank_hits <- ggplot(data, aes(Rank, Diff))+
    geom_point(aes(col=Group), alpha= 0.25)+
     scale_colour_manual(values=c('#e78ac3','#ffd92f', '#a6d854','#b3b3b3')
, labels=c('K27M-specific\nEssential', 'K27M-KO-specific\nEssential', 'Common Essential', 'Insubstantial Difference\nin Essentiality') )+
    geom_hline(aes(linetype='dashed',yintercept = threshold_pos_y), col= 'black', alpha = 0.2)+
    geom_hline(aes(linetype = 'dashed',yintercept = threshold_neg_y), col= 'black', alpha = 0.2) +
    scale_linetype_manual(labels=c('Top 100 Hit Threshold'), values=c('dashed'))+
    theme(legend.title=element_blank(),
          legend.spacing.y=unit(0.25,'cm'))+
    guides(colour = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
    geom_text_repel(aes(label=Label, col=Group), max.overlaps=30, force =5, force_pull=0.5, show.legend = FALSE)+
    labs(x = 'Rank',
         y = 'Difference in Beta Score',
         title = str_wrap(paste('MAGeCK rank plot indicates top differential hits in the H3K27M line compared to H3K27M-KO line'),width = 65),
         caption= 'Common essential genes sourced from DepMap Q1/22')
  
  print(rank_hits)
  
  #Exporting .tsv files
  write_tsv(diff_betas_cc,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_list.tsv',sep='_'))
  
  pos_selection_hundred <- diff_betas_cc %>%
    filter(Rank <= 100) %>%
    arrange(Rank) %>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(pos_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_pos_100_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  
  neg_selection_hundred <- diff_betas_cc %>%
    filter(Rank >= nrow(diff_betas_cc)-100) %>%
    arrange(-Rank)%>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(neg_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_neg_100_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  
  #For non-common essential only in top 100
    ce_filtered_pos_selection_hundred <- diff_betas_cc %>%
    filter(Rank <= 100) %>%
    filter(common_essential==FALSE) %>%
    arrange(Rank) %>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(ce_filtered_pos_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_pos_100_ce_filtered_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  
  ce_filtered_neg_selection_hundred <- diff_betas_cc %>%
    filter(Rank >= nrow(diff_betas_cc)-100) %>%
    filter(common_essential ==FALSE) %>%
    arrange(-Rank)%>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(ce_filtered_neg_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_neg_100_ce_filtered_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  

  return(list(differential_hits,rank_hits))
}


# differential_hits_plot <- differential_hits_plotter(mle_betas_cc, 'KO','CON', top = 10, sd_cutoff=2, prefix='DIPG') #For DIPG case, the CON H3K27M mutant is the cell line of interest on y-axis compared to the KO H3-KO mutant on x-axis
# 
# multiple_plot_exporter(differential_hits_plot, 'hit_plots')
# 
# rm(differential_hits_plot)

```

Below is the now-used variant of the above code but which implements the fdr significance cutoff instead of a quantile matching approach.

```{r diff_hits_fdr}
fdr_differential_hits_plotter <- function(data, x,y, top, fdr_cutoff, prefix){
  
  sample <- sym(y)
  ctrl  <- sym(x)
  slope <- 1
  
  
  data$common_essential[data$Gene == 'TWISTNB'] <- TRUE
  
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(Rank = rank(-Diff)) %>%
    mutate(rank_Label = case_when(Rank <= top ~ Gene,
                             Rank >= nrow(data)-top ~ Gene,
                             TRUE ~ '')) %>% 
    arrange(Rank)
  
  diff_betas_cc <<- data
    
  differential_hits <- ggplot(data, aes(!!ctrl, !!sample))+
    geom_point(aes(col=Group), alpha=0.25)+
    scale_colour_manual(values=c('#e78ac3','#ffd92f', '#a6d854','#b3b3b3')
, labels=c('K27M-specific\nEssential', 'K27M-KO-specific\nEssential', 'Common Essential', 'Insignificant Difference\nin Essentiality') )+
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col='black', linetype='dotted', alpha = 0.35)+
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col='black', linetype = 'dotted', alpha = 0.35)+
    geom_abline(aes(slope =1, intercept=0, linetype='dashed'), col='black', alpha=0.35)+
    scale_linetype_manual(labels=c('Equivalence of Beta\nin K27M and K27M-KO'), values=c('dashed'))+
    # geom_abline(slope =1 , intercept =max(intercept), col='black', linetype='dashed', alpha = 0.35)+
    # geom_abline(slope= 1, intercept = min(intercept), col='black', linetype = 'dashed', alpha = 0.35)+
    labs(x = 'Beta Scores of K27M-KO line',
         y ='Beta Scores of K27M line',
         title = str_wrap(paste('Comparison of beta indicates differential hits in the H3K27M line compared to H3K27M-KO line'),width = 65),
         caption= 'Common essential genes sourced from DepMap Q1/22')+
    theme(legend.title=element_blank(),
          legend.spacing.y=unit(0.25,'cm'))+
    guides(colour = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
    geom_label_repel(aes(label=Label, col=Group), max.overlaps = 100000, force =1,force_pull=0.5, show.legend = FALSE, max.time=5)+
    scale_y_continuous(expand=expansion(add=1))

  print(differential_hits)
  
  threshold_pos_y <- data$Diff[data$Rank==100]
  threshold_neg_y <- data$Diff[data$Rank == nrow(data)-100]
  
  rank_hits <- ggplot(data, aes(Rank, Diff))+
    geom_point(aes(col=Group), alpha= 0.25)+
    geom_hline(aes(linetype='dashed',yintercept = threshold_pos_y), col= 'black', alpha = 0.2)+
    geom_hline(aes(linetype = 'dashed',yintercept = threshold_neg_y), col= 'black', alpha = 0.2) +
    scale_linetype_manual(labels=c('Top 100 Hit Threshold'), values=c('dashed'))+
    scale_colour_manual(values=c('#e78ac3','#ffd92f', '#a6d854','#b3b3b3')
, labels=c('K27M-specific\nEssential', 'K27M-KO-specific\nEssential', 'Common Essential', 'Insignificant Difference\nin Essentiality') )+
    theme(legend.title=element_blank(),
          legend.spacing.y=unit(0.25,'cm'))+
    guides(colour = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
    geom_text_repel(aes(label=rank_Label, col=Group), max.overlaps = 100000, force =1,force_pull=0.5, show.legend = FALSE, max.time=5)+
    labs(x = 'Rank',
         y = 'Difference in Beta Score',
         title = str_wrap(paste('MAGeCK rank plot indicates top differential hits in the H3K27M line compared to H3K27M-KO line'),width = 65),
         caption= 'Common essential genes sourced from DepMap Q1/22')
  
  print(rank_hits)
  
  #Exporting .tsv files
  write_tsv(data,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_list.tsv',sep='_'))
  
  pos_selection_hundred <- data %>%
    filter(Group == 'Less Essential') %>%
    arrange(Rank) %>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(pos_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_pos_signif_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  
  neg_selection_hundred <- data %>%
    filter(Group == 'More Essential') %>%
    arrange(-Rank)%>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(neg_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_neg_signif_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  
  #For non-common essential 
    ce_filtered_pos_selection_hundred <- data %>%
    filter(Group == 'Less Essential') %>%
    filter(common_essential==FALSE) %>%
    arrange(Rank) %>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(ce_filtered_pos_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_pos_signif_ce_filtered_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  
  ce_filtered_neg_selection_hundred <- data %>%
    filter(Group == 'More Essential') %>%
    filter(common_essential ==FALSE) %>%
    arrange(-Rank)%>%
    select(Gene)
  
  write_tsv(ce_filtered_neg_selection_hundred,paste(prefix,'ranked_beta_neg_signif_ce_filtered_list.tsv',sep='_'),col_names = FALSE)
  

  return(list(differential_hits,rank_hits))
}


fdr_diff_hits_plot <- fdr_differential_hits_plotter(wald_diff_output_tb, 'KO','CON', top = 6, prefix='DIPG') #For DIPG case, the CON H3K27M mutant is the cell line of interest on y-axis compared to the KO H3-KO mutant on x-axis

multiple_plot_exporter(fdr_diff_hits_plot, 'fdr_hit_plots')

rm(fdr_diff_hits_plot)
```

### Individual Gene Boxplots

This section is to plot individual boxplots for normalised read counts over time for a series of genes to provide one summary figure of results including:
- Normalised read counts of nontargeting controls over time (combining replicates into single box)
- Normalised read counts of Depmap common essential over time (combining replicates into single box)
- Normalised read counts of ASCL1 over time (combining replicates into single box)
- Normalised read counts of BAMBI over time (combining replicates into single box)

```{r individual_gene_boxplots_QC}

#This plots individual boxplots of genes you specify in the function argument
individual_gene_boxplotter <- function(input_norm_counts, subset_gene_name, gene_selection='', sample_selection=''){
  
  if (subset_gene_name == 'NTC'){
    data_tb <- input_norm_counts %>%
      filter(is.na(Gene))
    main <- 'Normalised read counts of non-targeting controls are similar across timepoints'
    wilcox_side <- 'two.sided'
  } else if (subset_gene_name == 'CE'){
    data_tb <- input_norm_counts %>%
      filter(Gene %in% common_essentials$gene)
    main <- 'Normalised read counts of DepMap common essential genes decrease significantly over time'
    wilcox_side <- 'greater'
  } else {
    data_tb <- input_norm_counts %>%
      filter(Gene == subset_gene_name)
    
    indiv_gene <- TRUE
    
    main <- paste('Normalised read counts of', subset_gene_name, 'indicate', gene_selection,'over time in',sample_selection)
      if (str_detect(gene_selection,'essential')){
        wilcox_side <- 'greater'
      } else if (str_detect(gene_selection,'tsg')){
        wilcox_side <- 'less'
      }
  }
  
  #Merging replicates together
  data_tb <- data_tb %>%
    mutate(Plasmid2 = Plasmid) %>%
    rename(Plasmid1 = Plasmid) %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(sample=variable, count=value) %>%
    mutate(Replicate = case_when(str_detect(sample,'r1|Plasmid')~'1', str_detect(sample, 'r2')~'2')) %>%
    mutate(sample = case_when(
      str_detect(sample,'CON_r1_d0')~'CON_d0',
      str_detect(sample,'CON_r1_d11')~'CON_d11',
      str_detect(sample,'CON_r1_d21')~'CON_d21',
      str_detect(sample,'CON_r2_d0')~'CON_d0',
      str_detect(sample,'CON_r2_d11')~'CON_d11',
      str_detect(sample,'CON_r2_d21')~'CON_d21',
      str_detect(sample,'KO_r1_d0')~'KO_d0',
      str_detect(sample,'KO_r1_d11')~'KO_d11',
      str_detect(sample,'KO_r1_d21')~'KO_d21',
      str_detect(sample,'KO_r2_d0')~'KO_d0',
      str_detect(sample,'KO_r2_d11')~'KO_d11',
      str_detect(sample,'KO_r2_d21')~'KO_d21',
      str_detect(sample,'Plasmid1') ~ 'Plasmid1',
      str_detect(sample,'Plasmid2') ~ 'Plasmid2'
    )) %>%
    mutate(sample = factor(sample, levels = c('Plasmid1','CON_d0','CON_d11','CON_d21','Plasmid2','KO_d0','KO_d11','KO_d21'),ordered=TRUE)) %>%
    mutate(CellLine = case_when(str_detect(sample,'CON')~'K27M', str_detect(sample,'KO')~'K27M-KO', str_detect(sample,'Plasmid')~'Plasmid')) %>%
    mutate(Timepoint = case_when(str_detect(sample,'d0') ~ 'Day 0',
                                 str_detect(sample,'d11') ~ 'Day 11',
                                 str_detect(sample,'d21') ~ 'Day 21',
                                 str_detect(sample,'Plasmid') ~ 'Plasmid',)) 
  
  top_comparisons <- list(c('CON_d0','CON_d21'),c('KO_d0','KO_d21'))
  bot_comparisons <- list(c('CON_d0','CON_d11'),c('CON_d11','CON_d21'),
                          c('KO_d0','KO_d11'),c('KO_d11','KO_d21'))
  
  indiv_tb <<- data_tb
    #Code to include the individual gRNAs as separately coloured dots in geom_boxplot
    if (indiv_gene == TRUE){
      data_tb <- data_tb %>%
        arrange(sgRNA) %>%
        mutate(sgRNA_num = case_when(sgRNA == sgRNA[[1]] ~ '1',
                                     sgRNA == sgRNA[[nrow(data_tb)]] ~ '2',
                                     TRUE ~ '0'))
    }
  
    read_distribution <- ggplot(data_tb, aes(x=sample, y=count)) +
    geom_boxplot(aes(fill=CellLine), alpha=0.75) +
    geom_point(aes(col=sgRNA_num), alpha=1)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=top_comparisons
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative=wilcox_side), map_signif_level = TRUE, margin_top = 0.2)+
    geom_signif(comparisons=bot_comparisons
                  ,test=(wilcox.test),test.args =c(alternative=wilcox_side), map_signif_level = TRUE)+
    scale_fill_brewer(name='',type='qual',palette='Set2')+
    scale_colour_manual(name='gRNA', labels=c('1','2'), values=c('#ffd92f','#b3b3b3'))+
    labs(x='Timepoint', y='Counts per gRNA', 
       title=str_wrap(main, width =65),
       caption='Statistical test via one-sided Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test')+
    theme(axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-0.5),
          text=element_text(size=16))+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c('Plasmid','Day\n0','Day\n11', 'Day\n21',
                              'Plasmid','Day\n0','Day\n11', 'Day\n21'))
    
    #if(gene_selection != ''){
    #  read_distribution <- read_distribution +
    #    geom_point(aes(x=sample, y= count,col=Replicate),data = data_tb) +
    #    scale_colour_manual(labels=c('1','2'), values=c('#e5c494','#b3b3b3'))
    #  read_distribution$layers <- c(geom_point(),read_distribution$layers[[1]],read_distribution$layers[[2]],read_distribution$layers[[3]],read_distribution$layers[[4]])
    #}
    
    print(read_distribution)
    return(read_distribution)
    
}

#LMAO, YOU COULD TOTALLY DO ALL THESE WITH A SIMPLE MAP() FUNCTION

#ntc_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'NTC')
#depmap_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'CE')
PCGF2_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'PCGF2', 'essential','only the K27M line')
ASCL1_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'ASCL1','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#TCF3_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'TCF3','essential', 'only the K27M line')
BAMBI_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'BAMBI','essential', 'only the K27M line')
AHR_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'AHR','essential', 'only the K27M line')
ARNT_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'ARNT','essential', 'only the K27M line')

#Some tumour suppressor genes
PTEN_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'PTEN','tsg', 'only the K27M line')
CREBBP_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'CREBBP','tsg', 'only the K27M line')
TP53_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'TP53','tsg', 'only the K27M line')
#ARID2_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'ARID2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#TPK1_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts, 'TPK1','essential')
#MAP2K7_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts, 'MAP2K7','positive selection')
#TNFAIP6_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts, 'TNFAIP6','positive selection')
#KDSR_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts, 'KDSR','positive selection')
#HES5_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'HES5','essential')

CDKN2A_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'CDKN2A','essential', 'only the K27M line')

#Some previously-IDed DIPG-specific vulnerabilities
#BMI1_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'BMI1','essential', 'only the K27M line')
AFF4_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'AFF4','essential', 'only the K27M line')
BRD2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'BRD2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
BRD4_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'BRD4','essential', 'only the K27M line') # COMMON ESSENTIAL
#HDAC1_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'HDAC1','essential', 'only the K27M line')
HDAC2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'HDAC2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#KDM6A_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'KDM6A','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#KDM6B_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'KDM6B','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#EZH2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'EZH2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
EED_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'EED','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#SUZ12_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'SUZ12','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#RING1_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'RING1','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#RNF2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'RNF2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
MYC_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'MYC','essential', 'only the K27M line') #cOMMON ESSENTIAL
#SMYD2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'SMYD2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
NAMPT_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'NAMPT','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#NSD1_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'NSD1','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#NSD2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'NSD2','essential', 'only the K27M line')

## These genes are the enriched ones from GO
POLE3_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'POLE3','essential', 'only the K27M line')
POLE4_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'POLE4','essential', 'only the K27M line')
EIF3L_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'EIF3L','essential', 'only the K27M line')
EIF3K_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'EIF3K','essential', 'only the K27M line')


## These genes are significant K27M-specific essentials in only one of the two analysis pathways
#MAGeCK-only
MYH9_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'MYH9','essential', 'only the K27M line')
CYP27B1_summary<-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'CYP27B1','essential', 'only the K27M line')
METTL7B_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'METTL7B','essential', 'only the K27M line')
#BAGEL2-only
ARID2_summary <-individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'ARID2','essential', 'only the K27M line')
NPIPB9_summary <- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'NPIPB9','essential', 'only the K27M line')
RIPK3_summary<- individual_gene_boxplotter(norm_counts,'RIPK3','essential', 'only the K27M line')

individual_boxplots <- list(ASCL1_summary, BAMBI_summary, AHR_summary, ARNT_summary, PCGF2_summary)
multiple_plot_exporter(individual_boxplots, 'summary_read_boxplots')
rm(individual_boxplots, ntc_summary, depmap_summary,ASCL1_summary, BAMBI_summary, AHR_summary, ARNT_summary, PCGF2_summary)

previous_validated_boxplots <- list(AFF4_summary, BRD2_summary,HDAC2_summary, EED_summary)
multiple_plot_exporter(previous_validated_boxplots, 'previous_validated_boxplots')


```



## Functional Analysis of Hits

N.B This is deprecated + I decided to use ShinyGO instead!

The MAGeCKFlute R package enables use of numerous functional analysis modules e.g Gene Ontology (GO) term analysis and Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes & Genomes (KEGG) enrichment analysis.

Specifically, gene enrichment analysis is applied via the EnrichAB() function sourced from FluteMLE() function. This enables enrichment of genes into 'clusters' defined by various databases including:
1. KEGG (Kyoto Encylopedia of Genes & Genomes)
2. GOBP (Gene Ontology Biological Processes)
3. REACTOME (Reactome Biological Pathways)
4. COMPLEX (CORUM Database of Protein Complexes)

The genes identified as putative cell-line specific hits for positive and negative selection are arbitrarily defined via a beta-score cutoff of 2x standard deviations from the line y=x (as per the comparative plot earlier). These genes are enriched using the aforementioned databses into pathways/ontology via the following possible tests which can be defined in the EnrichAB function arguments:

1. ORT (Over-representation test which tests if genes from pre-defined clusters from databases are over-represented in a subset of the data e.g in database, representation of genes in nucleotide-excision repair = 2%, ORT tests if there is overrpresetation of NER-linked genes in the positive or negative selection putative hits)
2. GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis)
3. HGT (Hypergeometric Test)

```{r functional_analysis, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# scale_cutoff <- 2 #Default value for scale_cutoff in FluteMLE is 2x standard deviations for cutoff
# intercept = c(-CutoffCalling(diff_betas_cc$Diff, scale_cutoff),
#                   CutoffCalling(diff_betas_cc$Diff, scale_cutoff))
# 
# #Making EntrezID column name
# diff_betas_cc$EntrezID = TransGeneID(diff_betas_cc$Gene, 'Symbol', "Entrez", organism = 'hsa')
# 
# input_enrich_df <- ScatterView(diff_betas_cc, "CON", "KO", groups = c("top", "bottom"),
# 	                   groupnames = c("GroupA", "GroupB"), intercept = intercept)
# 
# 
# source('./EnrichAB.R') #Sourcing source code function of EnrichAB.R and noenrichplot() from EnrichedView.R from MAGeCKFlute package
# 
# enrichment_results <- EnrichAB(input_enrich_df$data,
#                                pvalue = 0.05, #For the pvalue cutoff for the statistical test used for enrichment
#                                enrich_method = 'ORT',
#                                limit = c(1,120), # Default values for min and max size of pathways
#                                filename = 'dipg')


enrichment_plotter <- function(enrich_data, database='KEGG', prefix){
  
  pos_name <- paste(tolower(database),'A',sep='')
  neg_name <- paste(tolower(database),'B',sep='')
  
   if (database != 'COMPLEX'){
    pos_title <- paste(database,'enrichment analysis shows clustering of positive selection hits into enriched pathways')
    neg_title <- paste(database,'enrichment analysis shows clustering of negative selection hits into complexes')
    
  } else if (database == 'COMPLEX'){
    pos_title <- paste(database,'enrichment analysis shows clustering of positive selection hits into enriched pathways')
    neg_title <- paste(database,'enrichment analysis shows clustering of negative selection hits into complexes')
  }
  
  #Convert positive and negative selection results to a tibble for ggplot
  if(!is.null(enrich_data[[pos_name]]$enrichRes)){
     pos_data <- as_tibble(enrich_data[[pos_name]]$enrichRes) %>%
    mutate(neglogpval = -log10(`p.adjust`))
     
    pos_plot <- ggplot(pos_data, aes(x=neglogpval, y=Description))+
    geom_point(aes(size=Count), col='blue')+
    xlab('-log10 (adjusted p value)')+
    labs(title=str_wrap(pos_title,width=60))+
    theme(axis.title.y=element_blank())
  
    print(pos_plot)

  } else{
    print('No positive selection hits')
  }
  
  if(!is.null(enrich_data[[neg_name]]$enrichRes)){
    neg_data <- as_tibble(enrich_data[[neg_name]]$enrichRes)%>%
    mutate(neglogpval = -log10(`p.adjust`))
    
    neg_plot <- ggplot(neg_data, aes(x=neglogpval, y=Description))+
    geom_point(aes(size=Count), col='blue')+
    xlab('-log10 (adjusted p value)')+
    labs(title=str_wrap(neg_title,width=60))+
    theme(axis.title.y=element_blank())
  
    print(neg_plot)
  } else{
    print('No negative selection hits')
  }
 
  
  plot_list <- list(neg_plot, pos_plot)
  multiple_plot_exporter(plot_list, paste(prefix,database,'enrichment_plots',sep='_'))
  
}

# enrichment_plotter(enrichment_results, 'KEGG', 'dipg')
# enrichment_plotter(enrichment_results, 'GOBP', 'dipg')
# enrichment_plotter(enrichment_results, 'REACTOME', 'dipg')
# enrichment_plotter(enrichment_results, 'COMPLEX', 'dipg')

```


### Plotter for ShinyGO Data
This is a short code to plot my personal ShinyGO data - not generalisable.

```{r shinygo_plotter}
k27m_gocc <- read_csv(paste(working_directory, 'GOCC_K27M.csv', sep="/"))

k27m_lollipop <- ggplot(k27m_gocc,aes(x=log10(`Fold Enrichment`), y=reorder(str_wrap(Pathway,width=25), -`Enrichment FDR`)))+
  geom_point(aes(col=`Enrichment FDR`))+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=log10(`Fold Enrichment`), y=str_wrap(Pathway,width=25), yend=str_wrap(Pathway,width=25), col=`Enrichment FDR`))+
  xlab(bquote(log[10]~'(Fold Enrichment)'))+
  ylab('Cellular Component')+
  scale_colour_distiller(palette='RdBu')+
  geom_label_repel(aes(label=Genes, col=`Enrichment FDR`))+
  theme(text=element_text(size=16))

print(k27m_lollipop)
ggsave('k27m_lollipop_gocc.png',k27m_lollipop, width = 9, height = 6, units='in')


ko_gocc <- read_csv(paste(working_directory, 'GOCC_K27M-KO.csv', sep="/")) %>%
  mutate(ID = row_number()) %>%
  filter(ID %in% c(1,3))

ko_lollipop <- ggplot(ko_gocc,aes(x=log10(`Fold Enrichment`), y=reorder(str_wrap(Pathway,width=25), -`Enrichment FDR`)))+
  geom_point(aes(col=`Enrichment FDR`))+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=log10(`Fold Enrichment`), y=str_wrap(Pathway,width=25), yend=str_wrap(Pathway,width=25), col=`Enrichment FDR`))+
  xlab(bquote(log[10]~'(Fold Enrichment)'))+
  ylab('Cellular Component')+
  scale_colour_distiller(palette='RdBu')+
  geom_label_repel(aes(label=Genes, col=`Enrichment FDR`))+
  theme(text=element_text(size=16))

print(ko_lollipop)
ggsave('k27m-ko_lollipop_gocc.png',ko_lollipop, width = 9, height = 6, units='in')
```

```{r terminus}
sessionInfo()
```
